## SECTION 19 — This Day in History (Global)
*   In 1863, the pivotal, three-day Civil War Battle of Gettysburg began in Pennsylvania, resulting in a Union victory.
*   In 1867, Canada became a self-governing dominion of Great Britain as the British North America Act took effect, a day now commemorated as Canada Day.
*   In 1903, the first Tour de France bicycle race began.
*   In 1916, the Battle of the Somme began, with 19,000 British Army soldiers killed and 40,000 wounded on the first day.
*   In 1944, delegates from 44 countries met at Bretton Woods, New Hampshire, to establish the International Monetary Fund and the World Bank.
*   In 1963, the U.S. Post Office inaugurated its five-digit ZIP codes.
*   In 1979, Sony introduced the Walkman, a portable audio cassette player, in Japan.
*   In 1991, the Warsaw Pact, a defense treaty between 8 communist countries, was formally disbanded in Prague.
*   In 1997, Hong Kong reverted to Chinese rule after 156 years as a British colony.
*   In 2002, the International Criminal Court (ICC) was established as the first international judicial body with the power to try individuals for genocide, crimes against humanity, and war crimes.

---

## SECTION 20 — This Day in History (India)
*   In 1821, the daily newspaper 'Mumbai Samachar' was published.
*   In 1862, the Calcutta High Court was inaugurated, becoming the oldest High Court in India.
*   In 1879, the first postcards were issued in India by the Post and Telegraph Department.
*   In 1933, Abdul Hamid, an Indian soldier posthumously awarded the Param Vir Chakra, was born.
*   In 1947, the British Parliament passed the India Independence Act, fixing August 15 for the transfer of power.
*   In 1949, the Institute of Chartered Accountants of India (ICAI) was formed, and this day is now celebrated as Chartered Accountants Day.
*   In 1955, the Imperial Bank of India was nationalized and renamed as the State Bank of India.
*   In 1961, Kalpana Chawla, the first Indian woman astronaut, was born.
*   In 1964, the Industrial Development Bank of India (IDBI) was established.
*   In 2017, the Goods and Services Tax (GST) was introduced in India, marking India's largest tax reform, and is now celebrated as GST Day.
